title: Runtime-Lens evidence bridge
goal: Define a bounded bridge between RepoBrief code evidence and Chronik/runtime observations so agents can cite code/runtime drift without collapsing snapshot authority into runtime truth.

acceptance.assertion: Runtime evidence sources, timestamps and authority labels are explicit and hashable or otherwise provenance-bound.
The bridge can report code/runtime drift as observation, not as correctness verdict.
RepoBrief does not own service restart, deploy, systemd, secrets or live runtime mutation.

validation: 253 Chronik tests and the role contract passed after hardening.
All GitHub checks including CodeQL passed.
The event now satisfies Chronik strict provenance through source.repo/source.component.
Forbidden actions and non-claims are required as complete sets rather than optional subsets.

verified_acceptance.rpu-v1-t017-runtime-inputs: Runtime source, observed timestamp, authority label and digest/URI provenance are required.
verified_acceptance.rpu-v1-t017-drift-report: Drift is observation-only and verdict_authority is fixed to none.
verified_acceptance.rpu-v1-t017-boundary: The contract prohibits RepoBrief runtime/Git mutations and Chronik dispatch, deploy or verdict authority.

metadata.outcome: A bounded Runtime-Lens observation contract links RepoBrief code evidence and runtime observations without creating correctness or execution authority.
